schema_changelog_1.2.0: added spec_patch_2026_05_01_sha256 field for pre-M6 amendment lock (2026-05-01 session 3)

halts.trigger_class: T11 (patch §11.7) — sign-prediction failure on advance-only delayed_chain
halts.severity: paper-critical / BLOCKER semantics per addendum §6 + patch §11.7
halts.reason: test_smoke_DC_Long50_AUC_ordering fails by construction: Discrete(1) advance-only chain has zero variance in returns regardless of beta, so AUC(+1)=AUC(0)=AUC(-1)=1000.0; patch §11.3 P-Sign prediction not testable in current implementation form
halts.resolution: v3 patch tasks/phase_VIII_spec_patches_2026-05-01_v3_T11_resolution.md applied option (a); metric switched to q_convergence_rate; metric scaffolding sound but PREDICTION SIGN STILL WRONG → second halt below

halts.trigger_class: T11 (spec §5.7 v4) — metric design flaw; β=0 dominates on classical-Q* convergence axis
halts.severity: paper-critical / BLOCKER per addendum §6 + v4 directive
halts.reason: v4 sign-flip + q_star off-by-one fix + terminal-state slice ALL applied correctly; metric scaffolding sound (47 PASS). Smoke test STILL fails because the prediction is on the wrong axis: classical Q* is the fixed point of T_{β=0} only, not T_{β≠0}. Empirical: AUC(rate(0))=21.03 (full convergence to classical Q*, residual hits eps floor) > AUC(rate(-1))=3.58 (overshoots, lands at β-specific fixed point ~0.55) > AUC(rate(+1))=-27.87 (diverges to ~1e13). β=0 dominates because only β=0 has classical Q* as its fixed point. v3 + v4 sign flips were both wrong because the underlying metric design assumes a β-monotonic ordering that doesn't hold (TAB operator fixed point shifts with β).
remediation_options: (δ) switch metric to Bellman-residual decay; rewrite smoke
(ε) drop the absolute prediction; keep cell as informational diagnostic
halts.orchestrator_recommendation: (δ) — most paper-aligned; would need new helper + smoke rewrite + empirical verification

halts.severity: paper-critical / BLOCKER semantics per addendum §6 + v3 patch §1 step 5
halts.reason: v3 patch metric (q_convergence_rate) is correct and tests pass; but the v3-rewritten smoke test reveals patch §11.3 P-Contract claim has signs reversed: empirically AUC(rate(-1))≈+27.87 > AUC(rate(0))≈0 > AUC(rate(+1))≈-27.87. With optimistic Q-init Q_0=1/(1-γ)=20 on advance-only chain, alignment condition β·(r-v)≥0 with r=0<v requires β≤0 (NEGATIVE β tightens). The TAB target asymptotic g_{β,γ}(0,v) → (1+γ)·v as β→+∞ (NOT γ·v as v2 patch §11.3 incorrectly claimed), so +β amplifies the optimistic bootstrap and Q diverges from Q*.
remediation_options: (α) flip P-Contract sign — orchestrator recommends; trivial; preserves long-horizon evidence
(β) switch to pessimistic Q-init — paper-alignment weak; pessimistic init unusual
(γ) drop advance-only subcases entirely — loses P-Scaling test; weakest

next_action.details: THIRD HALT at M2 reopen close after v4 sign flip + q_star off-by-one + terminal-state slice all applied. v4 metric scaffolding sound; alignment-condition derivation correct; off-by-one fixed; terminal slice exposed deeper issue. Empirical: β=0 fully converges to classical Q* (AUC=21.03); β=-1 overshoots toward β-specific fixed point ~0 and lands at ~0.55 (AUC=3.58); β=+1 diverges to ~1e13 (AUC=-27.87). Neither v3 (+/0/-) nor v4 (-/0/+) ordering matches; β=0 dominates because only β=0 has classical Q* as its fixed point. Metric design flaw: q_convergence_rate(q_history, q_star_classical) implicitly assumes all β share classical Q* as fixed point, but TAB fixed point shifts with β. Per v4 directive: 'Do NOT auto-fix beyond v4'. Halt memo at results/adaptive_beta/tab_six_games/halts/delayed_chain_metric_design_flaw_2026-05-01T11-31-32Z.md enumerates 3 remediation options (δ/ε/ζ); orchestrator recommends (δ) Bellman-residual decay (use existing contraction_reward helper from spec §M5). Working tree retains v4 corrections pending researcher decision.
